description: We administer the legal aid scheme in England and Wales, in accordance with the Legal Aid, Sentencing and Punishment of Offenders Act 2012. LAA is an executive agency, sponsored by the Ministry of Justice .

details.body:
We administer the legal aid scheme in England and Wales, in accordance with the Legal Aid, Sentencing and Punishment of Offenders Act 2012.

LAA is an executive agency, sponsored by the Ministry of Justice.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: We have launched a survey for all providers who currently hold a live legal aid contract. The survey opened on 27 April 2026 and will close on 1 June 2026.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Legal Aid Provider Survey 2026

ordered_featured_documents.summary: A new eligibility key card for 2026 is now available.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Civil Eligibility Keycard 2026

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Local authorities across England are receiving an extra £41 million to cover costs for new enforcement powers through the landmark Renters’ Rights Act.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Councils backed with millions to take on rogue landlords

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Peer Reviewer contract holders will receive contract extension from 1 April 2025.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Extension notices to be issued for peer reviewer contracts

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Information for individuals interested in applying to deliver Peer Review Independent Expert Panel Services from June 2026.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Tender Opens for Peer Review Independent Expert Panel

ordered_featured_documents.summary: We are seeking more capacity to ensure clients in Trafford can continue to access prompt family legal aid services.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Increasing family legal aid providers in Trafford

ordered_featured_links.title: Check if you can get legal aid

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use.

details.body:
The chief executive provides overall leadership of the LAA and works to ensure that legal aid is provided effectively and efficiently in England and Wales. Responsibilities include:

  • Legal aid case management
  • Legal aid commissioning and strategy
  • Legal aid finance and digital
  • LAA corporate central functions
The chief executive provides overall leadership of the LAA and works to ensure that legal aid is provided effectively and efficiently in England and Wales.

The Head of Criminal Casework leads the teams that process all applications and bills for criminal legal aid and central funds. This also includes the collection and enforcement of contributions made under the Crown Court means testing scheme.

The post-holder is a member of the Executive Leadership Team, chairs the LAA’s Operational Committee and has membership of the LAA board.

role_appointments.title: Jane Harbottle - Head of Criminal Casework

details.body:
The Head of Civil Legal Aid is responsible for ensuring civil certificated applications and bills are processed effectively.

The post-holder leads teams processing civil applications, bills, payments on account, customer services, complaints handling, stakeholder training in civil processes, administration and the operation of the LAA national filing centre.

role_appointments.title: Jane Harbottle - Head of Civil Legal Aid

details.body:
The Deputy Director, Corporate Services is responsible for all corporate functions to ensure the safe and effective running of the agency.  The Head of Corporate Services is responsible for: 

  • Strategic planning including the LAA Strategy and Business Plan 
  • The LAA Strategic Workforce Plan and People Strategy
  • Performance reporting and analysis
  • Internal and external communications and strategic stakeholder engagement
  • Risk management and Assurance
  • Corporate Governance
  • Information security, Health & Safety and business continuity

role_appointments.title: Lucy Jones - Head of Corporate Services

details.body:
The Deputy Director, Corporate Services is responsible for all corporate functions to ensure the safe and effective running of the agency.  The Head of Corporate Services is responsible for: 

  • Strategic planning including the LAA Strategy and Business Plan 
  • The LAA Strategic Workforce Plan and People Strategy
  • Performance reporting and analysis
  • Internal and external communications and strategic stakeholder engagement
  • Risk management and Assurance
  • Corporate Governance
  • Information security, Health & Safety and business continuity

The Head of the Public Defender Service provides leadership for the PDS and has overall responsibility for PDS performance. The PDS is responsible for delivery of high quality defence representation in all types of criminal cases.

role_appointments.title: Malcolm Bryant - Head of Public Defender Service

details.body: The post-holder heads up an operational team which makes determinations for legal aid. This includes high profile and complex civil cases. The team sits within the LAA’s case management directorate.

role.title: Head of High Cost Civil Complex Cases

role_appointments.title: Malcolm Bryant - Head of High Cost Civil Complex Cases

details.body:
The Head of Service Development and Central Commissioning is responsible for:

  • policy implementation, working closely with the Ministry of Justice legal aid policy team

  • procurement of contracts for the Legal Aid Agency

role.title: Head of Service Development and Central Commissioning

role_appointments.title: David Phillips - Head of Service Development and Central Commissioning

details.body: The Head of Contract Management and Assurance is responsible for managing provider relationships, ensuring contract compliance and monitoring error levels within the legal aid fund. This includes identifying error reduction opportunities, compliance audit and quality assurance of legal aid providers. The team also includes the LAA’s counter fraud and investigations functions.

role.title: Head of Contract Management and Assurance

role_appointments.title: David Thomas - Head of Contract Management and Assurance

details.body:
The Head of Assurance’s responsibilities include;

  • monitoring of error levels within the legal aid fund
  • identifying error recovery opportunities
  • quality assurance of legal aid providers
  • corporate risk and assurance frameworks
  • counter-fraud and investigations functions

The Head of Case Management is responsible for ensuring applications and bills are processed effectively.

This includes leading teams dealing with applications, bills, payments on account, customer services, complaints handling, stakeholder training, administration and the operation of the LAA national filing centre. Also, the collection and enforcement of contributions made under the Crown Court means testing scheme.

The role includes membership of both the Executive Leadership Team and the LAA Board as well as chairing the LAA’s Operational Committee.

role_appointments.title: Alistair Adan - Interim Head of Case Management

details.body:
The Head of Transformation leads the transformation team, which is made up of Legal Aid Agency (LAA) staff and members of the project delivery function.

The vision of the transformation team is to lead the transformation of the LAA, through innovation, collaboration and inclusion, creating a better experience for everyone.

The team works closely with LAA Digital and teams across the LAA to implement change. This includes improvements to our digital services, as well as implementing policy changes.

The Head of Transformation leads the transformation team, which is made up of Legal Aid Agency (LAA) staff and members of the project delivery function.

The vision of the transformation team is to lead the transformation of the LAA, through innovation, collaboration and inclusion, creating a better experience for everyone.

The team works closely with LAA Digital and teams across the LAA to implement change. This includes improvements to our digital services, as well as implementing policy changes.

role_appointments.title: Hannah Payne - Head of Transformation, jobshare

details.title: Criminal billing enquiries

details.description:
Get free and confidential legal advice in England and Wales if you’re eligible for legal aid. Work out if you qualify for legal aid at https://www.gov.uk/check-legal-aid

The service will check if you qualify for legal aid and if you do you can speak to a CLA operator who will check your details and put you through to a CLA legal adviser.
details.title: Civil Legal Advice

details.description: The Public Defender Service (PDS) is a criminal law supplier, funded by the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) and has offices in Cheltenham, Darlington, Pontypridd and Swansea.
details.title: Public Defender Service

details.description: Our criminal defence barristers provide representation for legal aid clients in criminal cases and also work as court appointed advocates.
details.title: Public Defender Service: Advocacy Team

details.description: All enquiries relating to statutory charge cases or debts that have a security against a property. To assist us with your enquiry, please provide an Office Copy Entry alongside any correspondence, if you have one, and provide written authority if you are acting for a third party.
details.title: Secured Debt

details.description: Enquiries relating to management of unsecured debt and enquiries relating to receipt of money to the LAA. Payments can take up to 5 working days to show on our systems.
details.title: Legal Aid Agency Recovery Services

details.body: The director’s responsibilities include commissioning and contract management of legal aid providers throughout England and Wales, leading the Legal Aid Transformation programme and overseeing the Public Defender Service.

ordered_roles.title: Director, Legal Aid Commissioning and Strategy

details.body: The Director of Human Resources is responsible for the development and implementation of the ‘people strategy’ for the agency.

As well as leading the IT and digital department, this role leads on the design of digital public services, oversees how those services are managed and supported and heads up transformation programmes.

The Deputy Director, Corporate Services is responsible for all corporate functions to ensure the safe and effective running of the agency.  The Head of Corporate Services is responsible for: 

  • Strategic planning including the LAA Strategy and Business Plan 
  • The LAA Strategic Workforce Plan and People Strategy
  • Performance reporting and analysis
  • Internal and external communications and strategic stakeholder engagement
  • Risk management and Assurance
  • Corporate Governance
  • Information security, Health & Safety and business continuity
The chief executive provides overall leadership of the LAA and works to ensure that legal aid is provided effectively and efficiently in England and Wales.

Responsible for assuring the Director of Legal Aid Casework that our funding decisions are correct. Also, defending any proceedings brought against the Legal Aid Agency or Lord Chancellor in relation to the operation of the legal aid scheme.

The chief executive provides overall leadership of the LAA and works to ensure that legal aid is provided effectively and efficiently in England and Wales. Responsibilities include:

  • Legal aid case management
  • Legal aid commissioning and strategy
  • Legal aid finance and digital
  • LAA corporate central functions

ordered_roles.title: Chair of Legal Aid Agency Board

details.body: The director’s responsibilities include providing business partnering support to the Ministry of Justice’s corporate services functions and three executive agencies – the largest of which is the Legal Aid Agency.

The Head of Contract Management leads a team of around 100 people in 11 locations. The post-holder is responsible for managing relationships with legal aid providers and ensuring that they comply with contractual requirements.

The Head of Civil Legal Aid is responsible for ensuring civil certificated applications and bills are processed effectively.

The post-holder leads teams processing civil applications, bills, payments on account, customer services, complaints handling, stakeholder training in civil processes, administration and the operation of the LAA national filing centre.

The Head of Service Development and Central Commissioning is responsible for:

  • policy implementation, working closely with the Ministry of Justice legal aid policy team

  • procurement of contracts for the Legal Aid Agency

ordered_roles.title: Head of Service Development and Central Commissioning

details.body: The Head of the Public Defender Service provides leadership for the PDS and has overall responsibility for PDS performance. The PDS is responsible for delivery of high quality defence representation in all types of criminal cases.

The Head of Criminal Casework leads the teams that process all applications and bills for criminal legal aid and central funds. This also includes the collection and enforcement of contributions made under the Crown Court means testing scheme.

The post-holder is a member of the Executive Leadership Team, chairs the LAA’s Operational Committee and has membership of the LAA board.
The post-holder heads up an operational team which makes determinations for legal aid. This includes high profile and complex civil cases. The team sits within the LAA’s case management directorate.

ordered_roles.title: Head of High Cost Civil Complex Cases

details.body: The Deputy Director for Service Development and Central Commissioning is responsible for policy implementation, working closely with the Ministry of Justice Legal Aid Policy team, and the commissioning of legal aid services for the Legal Aid Agency.

ordered_roles.title: Joint Deputy Director, Service Development and Central Commissioning

details.body: The Head of Performance, Planning and Change is responsible for the LAA’s Strategy and Business Plan and supporting the Performance Framework. Also, performance monitoring across the organisation, corporate reporting, and the Agency’s significant portfolio of organisational change.

ordered_roles.title: Head of Performance, Planning and Change

details.body:
The Head of Assurance’s responsibilities include;

  • monitoring of error levels within the legal aid fund
  • identifying error recovery opportunities
  • quality assurance of legal aid providers
  • corporate risk and assurance frameworks
  • counter-fraud and investigations functions

The Deputy Director of Finance manages a team working in key areas for the delivery of business objectives. These include:

  1. Financial accounting, including the production of the annual statutory accounts
  2. Management accounting, including measuring performance against budgeted values
  3. Fund forecasting
  4. Business partnering
  5. Statistics
  6. Recovery services
The postholder leads a team providing business partnering support to the Legal Aid Agency, Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority, and the Office of the Public Guardian.

ordered_roles.title: Head of Operations Finance Business Partnering

details.body: The Head of Contract Management and Assurance is responsible for managing provider relationships, ensuring contract compliance and monitoring error levels within the legal aid fund. This includes identifying error reduction opportunities, compliance audit and quality assurance of legal aid providers. The team also includes the LAA’s counter fraud and investigations functions.

ordered_roles.title: Head of Contract Management and Assurance

details.body:
The Head of Case Management is responsible for ensuring applications and bills are processed effectively.

This includes leading teams dealing with applications, bills, payments on account, customer services, complaints handling, stakeholder training, administration and the operation of the LAA national filing centre. Also, the collection and enforcement of contributions made under the Crown Court means testing scheme.

The role includes membership of both the Executive Leadership Team and the LAA Board as well as chairing the LAA’s Operational Committee.

The Second Permanent Secretary works closely with the Permanent Secretary on all aspects of Ministry of Justice leadership. Jo holds responsibility for Her Majesty’s Prison and Probation Service and oversight for Legal Aid Agency, Official Solicitor and Public Trustee, Office of the Public Guardian and Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority.

The CEO of HM Prison and Probation Service has the strategic management and accountability of the agency and is a member of Ministry of Justice Departmental Board executive management committee.

ordered_roles.title: Second Permanent Secretary, Ministry of Justice and Chief Executive Officer, HM Prison & Probation Service

details.body:
The Head of Transformation leads the transformation team, which is made up of Legal Aid Agency (LAA) staff and members of the project delivery function.

The vision of the transformation team is to lead the transformation of the LAA, through innovation, collaboration and inclusion, creating a better experience for everyone.

The team works closely with LAA Digital and teams across the LAA to implement change. This includes improvements to our digital services, as well as implementing policy changes.
